records.ety: [OE. browe, bruwe, AS. brū; akin to AS. brǣw, brew, eyelid, OFries. brē, D. braauw, Icel. brā, brūn, OHG. prāwa, G. braue, OSlav. brv, Russ. brove, Ir. brai, Ir. & Gael. abhra, Armor. abrant, Gr. 'ofry`s, Skr. bhrū. Cf. Bray a bank, Bridge.]

defs: The prominent ridge over the eye, with the hair that covers it, forming an arch above the orbit.
quotes: And his arched brow, pulled o'er his eyes, With solemn proof proclaims him wise.

defs: The hair that covers the brow (ridge over the eyes); the eyebrow.
quotes: 'T is not your inky brows, your brack silk hair.

defs: The forehead; as, a feverish brow.
quotes: Beads of sweat have stood upon thy brow.

defs: The general air of the countenance.
quotes: To whom thus Satan with contemptuous brow.
He told them with a masterly brow.

defs: The edge or projecting upper part of a steep place; as, the brow of a precipice; the brow of a hill.
To bound to limit; to be at, or form, the edge of.
quotes: Tending my flocks hard by i' the hilly crofts That brow this bottom glade.
